mappings.rationale: Directly covers the adaptive significance of sex, sex patterns, sexual reproduction, and the evolution of reproduction and variation control.
Addresses sex and sexuality, the distinctions between sex, sexuality, and reproduction, and sex determination.
Covers sex chromosomes, genetic recombination, and the molecular basis of sexual and asexual reproduction.
Addresses reproductive system development, gamete formation, fertilization, and the life cycles of plants and animals.
Covers meiosis (division into gametes), binary fission, and cell-level reproduction fundamental to understanding reproduction.
Covers the reproductive system in plants including sex organs of bryophytes, variations in tracheophyte reproduction, and pollination.
Addresses hormonal influences on reproduction including sex hormones and their role in reproductive cycles in animals.
Covers natural selection and reproduction, the evolution of sex, and the establishment of genetic diversity through sexual reproduction.
Covers the diversity of reproductive systems across invertebrate and vertebrate animals, including gonads and reproductive specializations.
Addresses the genetic basis of sex determination, sex chromosomes, and the relationship between reproduction and hereditary transmission.
Covers fungal reproductive strategies including both sexual and asexual reproduction, relevant to the diversity of reproductive forms.
